agent_reply: Implemented `MostRecentFiles::get()` in [MostRecentFiles.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/MostRecentFiles.cpp) and replaced the TODO comment in [MostRecentFiles.h](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/MostRecentFiles.h).

It now returns a fixed `FileList` containing up to 6 root-directory file names, sorted newest-first by creation date/time, filtering to regular files with `FS_ATTRIB_ARCHIVE`. It scans once and keeps only the top six candidates.

Verification: I tried `pio run`. It failed before reaching this new file because of an existing `SdCard` namespace collision with SdFat’s `SdCard` typedef in `src/mic_sd_recorder_demo.cpp` / `lib/SdCard/SdCard.h`.

user_prompt: Thanks, that worked, I tested it with a quick call to my voicemail
Can you make `lib\AudioFileRecorder\MostRecentFiles.cpp` dynamically sized? Not like linked list, but with a initializer that allocates memory and a destructor that frees memory.
agent_reply: Done. `MostRecentFiles::FileList` now owns a dynamically allocated name table via `init()` and releases it in the destructor, with copy/move handling so returning from `get()` stays safe.

I also changed `get()` to allocate its candidate buffer dynamically instead of using a fixed stack array, and added `get(size_t maxFiles = kDefaultMaxFiles)` so callers can request a different capacity while existing `get()` calls still work.

commit_message: most recent file lister now dynamic

user_prompt: under security level 0, `lib\AudioFileRecorder\MostRecentFiles.cpp` should only list `*.wav` files. under higher security levels, both `*.wav` and `*.rec` files are listed. the number of files that is being listed should be controlled by one number inside `inc\conf.h`, set it to 20 now.
agent_reply: `MostRecentFiles` uses that as its default list size, and it now filters by security level:
I only changed `ScrollView` to remove the hard-coded `16` from `MostRecentFiles::get(16)`, leaving its unknown-file rendering path intact.
Both builds passed. Sec1 emitted a third-party FTP deprecation warning, but no build failure.

commit_message: bigger file list, with file type filters

Modify `MostRecentFiles` so that each `Candidate` contains a `file_size`, in bytes. And then the actual `FileList` also contains a `file_size` member.

`FileScrollItem` is initialized with knowledge about the `file_size`, and is responsible to calculate it into an estimated playback duration using the file size and file format. It generates a string internally as "<file size> / <duration HH:mm:SS>", and this can be read by `void ScrollView::drawSelectedText()` to be displayed underneath its label.

`FileScrollItem` now takes `fileSize` during `configureFile(...)`, formats an internal detail string like:
`ScrollView::drawSelectedText()` now keeps `scrollItem->label()` as the real filename, but renders the optional detail line underneath it when present.
